summary: Parallel review: FAIL (3 critical, 1 warning, 6 suggestion). Compound gate: WARN

findingsList.text: No 🔴 or 🟡 findings. `hasSimplicityVeto` is a clean pure function with proper null/non-array/missing-text guards. Tests (7 cases) cover empty/null/undefined, veto-present, plain `[simplicity]`, non-simplicity 🔴, parsed merged round-trip, and immutability — all 34 tests in `synthesize.test.mjs` pass. Wiring at `run.mjs:1318` correctly forces FAIL and bumps `synth.critical` for handshake consistency. Eval written to `eval-engineer.md`.

🔵 bin/lib/run.mjs:1318 — Defensive branch is unreachable while `computeVerdict` auto-FAILs on any 🔴; consider adding an integration test that fakes a non-FAIL synth verdict containing a `[simplicity veto]` line so this branch is regression-guarded.

The comment at `run.mjs:1314-1316` honestly acknowledges the redundancy with existing "any 🔴 → FAIL" behavior, which is the right call: making the contract explicit beats relying on an unstated coincidence.
